LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302 - RESPONDENTS THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, SEAN SELEGUE, ESTHER LIN, DONNA HERSHKOWITZ, COMMITTEE OF BAR EXAMINERS' DEMURRER IS OFF CALENDAR. AS PETITIONER NARAYAN BHANDARI'S OPPOSITION EFFECTIVELY CONCEDES, THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT HAS SOLE ORIGINAL JURISDICTION OVER THE ATTORNEY ADMISSIONS PROCESS. (SMITH V. STATE BAR (1989) 212 CAL.APP.3D 971, 976.) THUS, BHANDARI'S WRIT PETITION IS ORDERED TRANSFERRED TO THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT. (SEE CCP 396.) WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM.

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER
Off Calendar Jan-15-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances by ZOOM Video-conferencing:

Self-represented petitioner, Narayan Prasad Bhandari (Tel: 213.840.8119; email: oursangam@yahoo.com) appears on his own behalf as moving party

Attorney, Sean T. Strauss (email: sean.strauss@calbar.ca.gov), of the law offices of STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA OFFICE OF GENERAL COUNSEL (Tel: 415.538.2369) appears as counsel for defendants/opposing parties, THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, COMMITTEE OF BAR EXAMINERS,
SEAN SELEGUE, ESTHER P. LIN, and DONNA SUE HERSHKOWITZ

On calendar for hearing:

RESPONDENTS THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, SEAN SELEGUE, ESTHER LIN, DONNA HERSHKOWITZ, COMMITTEE OF BAR EXAMINERS' DEMURRER

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides, on record as reflected in the notes of the certified shorthand reporter.

The demurrer is off calendar. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

As petitioner Narayan Bhandari's opposition effectively concedes, the California Supreme Court has sole original jurisdiction over the attorney admissions process. (Smith v. State Bar (1989) 212 Cal.App.3d 971, 976.) Thus, Bhandari's writ petition is ordered transferred to the California Supreme Court. (See CCP 396.)

As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

Written order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com.
